subject: Add stretch goal UI to Campaign progress bar
description:
From stretch.md: "Once a user hits 100%, automatically animate the progress bar extending to a new tier." This addresses the Bystander Effect - donors bounce when campaigns look fully funded.

Implementation:
- When `raised >= goal`, show the original goal as "met" with a checkmark, then render a second progress segment toward a stretch goal (e.g., 125% or 150% of original)
- Add stretch goal data to campaigns.json for the one campaign closest to goal (campaign-2 at 81%)
- Animate the transition with a subtle pulse or glow
- Show tier labels: "Goal reached! Stretch: $250K unlocks expanded installation"

Fits naturally into CampaignPage.jsx donate panel. Small scope - one file change plus a fixture update.

changes: Added stretchGoal field to campaign-1 and campaign-2 in campaigns.json
Rendered stretch goal card with amber styling in donate panel, shows secondary progress bar when goal exceeded
